stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Hedy Lamarr (1914–2000) was a Hollywood film actress.
  • She was known for her glamorous on-screen presence and her work outside of film as an inventor.
  • In 2014, Lamarr was inducted into the National Inventors Hall of Fame for her major contribution to wireless technology.
  • According to film critic Yvonne Tasker, “Lamarr could play sophistication and danger in the same glance.”
  • According to engineer David Hughes, “Lamarr is the unsung pioneer of modern frequency-hopping communications.”
stem: The student wants to use a quotation to support a claim about Lamarr's contribution to wireless technology. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Hollywood actress Hedy Lamarr had a major contribution to wireless technology, and she was known for her glamorous on-screen presence.
Hedy Lamarr had such a major contribution to wireless technology that David Hughes called her “the unsung pioneer” of modern frequency-hopping communications.
David Hughes believes that Hedy Lamarr made a major contribution to wireless technology.
A Hollywood actress, Hedy Lamarr could “play sophistication and danger in the same glance,” according to Yvonne Tasker.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence uses the quotation from Hughes to support a claim about Lamarr's contribution to wireless technology, noting that the impact Lamarr had on the field led Hughes to call her “the unsung pioneer of modern frequency-hopping communications.”

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence makes the claim that Lamarr had a major contribution to wireless technology, it does not use a quotation to support this claim. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence presents Hughes's opinion that Lamarr made a major contribution but does not use a quotation to support the claim. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence includes a quotation about Lamarr's on-screen presence, it does not support a claim about her contribution to wireless technology.
